title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘summer-like warmth’ into the weekend
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston experiences summer-like temperatures reaching 90°F, with cooler coastal conditions due to onshore winds.
Meteorologists highlight the absence of humidity and storms, predicting warm weather to continue through the weekend.
Northern lights may be visible in northern New England tonight, offering a rare night-sky event.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (June 4, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Bey, a new Lebanese restaurant by the Samaha family, opens at 280 Shawmut Ave, South End.
Kowloon Tiki on the Beach debuts at 21 Revere Beach Blvd, offering tropical-themed dishes and drinks.
Buitoni Spaghetti Bar set to open in Kenmore Square, serving casual pasta dishes next year.

title: Payton Tolle’s emergence has been a bright spot amid Red Sox’ uneven 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched six scoreless innings in the Red Sox's 8-1 win over the Orioles at Fenway Park.
Tolle has a 2.28 ERA over eight starts this season, showcasing his effective fastball and command on the mound.
Tolle is the first Red Sox pitcher since 2018 to start the season with eight starts allowing three or fewer runs.

title: Police investigating after $30,000 in landscaping equipment stolen from community college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: $30,000 in landscaping equipment stolen from Massasoit Community College in three robberies over two days.
Thieves used a stolen silver Ford F-150 as the getaway vehicle; it was taken from a Brockton home.
Police pursued the suspects but they evaded capture; police request public assistance for information.

title: Hull police prepare for planned ‘large gathering’ at Nantasket Beach
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Hull police will increase staffing at Nantasket Beach due to potential large gathering flagged by social media.
Police Chief John Dunn expressed zero tolerance for criminal acts, alcohol on the beach, and disorderly conduct.
Recent disturbances at New England beaches, including in Rhode Island and New Hampshire, prompted similar police responses.

title: American flag burned, then replaced outside Masonic temple in Maine, fire officials says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An American flag was intentionally set on fire and destroyed outside a Masonic temple in Bath, Maine.
A replacement flag was found zip tied to the temple's entrance railing before firefighters arrived.
The Maine Fire Marshal's office is investigating the arson and searching for those responsible for replacing the flag.

title: Family of Mass. worker who died after getting trapped in freezer gets nearly $13 million in settlement
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts worker's family receives nearly $13 million settlement after freezer entrapment death.
Freezer door handle malfunction was central issue in wrongful death litigation.
Extensive investigation and expert testimony were utilized to resolve the case without trial.

title: Clover plans to reopen some locations after sudden closure, thanks to an anonymous investor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Clover Food Lab to reopen Cambridge and Boston locations on June 9 after securing anonymous investor support.
The chain initially announced closure of all 11 restaurants due to economic pressures and unsuccessful sale attempts.
CEO Julia Wrin Piper says Clover will focus on core Boston and Cambridge communities, reducing its operational footprint.

title: Abreu has a two-run homer and 3 RBIs, Tolle pitches 6 scoreless innings, and Red Sox beat Orioles 8-1
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle threw six scoreless innings, allowing seven hits and striking out five against the Orioles.
Wilyer Abreu hit a two-run homer and had three RBIs, contributing significantly to the Red Sox's 8-1 win.
Ceddanne Rafaela, Mickey Gasper, and Jarren Duran also contributed with hits, continuing Boston's strong offensive performance.

title: MassDems accuse GOP candidates of intentionally submitting false signatures to make primary ballot
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts Democrats accused GOP candidates Mike Walsh and Anne Manning-Martin of submitting fraudulent signatures for the September primary ballot.
Democratic Party officials urged Walsh and Manning-Martin to withdraw, alleging signatures were ineligible or fake, affecting their ballot eligibility.
The State Ballot Law Commission may hold hearings to verify signatures; insufficient valid signatures could lead to removal from the ballot.
